commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=77f985c70528b659b0584f493c2db54e19dcbcfc)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a configurable 120s per-task timeout to deferred context-engine turn maintenance, cancels the task record on timeout, throttles repeated `session.stalled` diagnostics, and adds focused tests plus config schema metadata.

PR surface: Source +83, Tests +171. Total +254 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main enqueues deferred turn maintenance without task-timeout options while the command queue supports them. I did not run a live lossless-claw lock-contention reproduction, so the exact production stall remains issue-log-backed rather than locally reproduced here.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs human follow-up because contributor proof is missing and the remaining blockers include timeout/fencing design plus acceptance of a new default/config surface.

target.title: fix(context-engine): bound deferred turn maintenance with a per-task timeout (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

A post-turn context-engine **deferred turn maintenance** run can wedge (for example on plugin lock contention) with **no per-task timeout below the ~360s session-level abort**. While it is wedged it holds its maintenance lane, so a user message queued behind it on the same session can wait minutes before being processed, even though the model is healthy. The gateway reports `active_work_without_progress` / `recovery=none` for the whole window, and `session.stalled` re-warns on every ~30s heartbeat with no backoff, spamming the logs.

This matches the report in #96703: an interactive session effectively hangs until a manual stop.

## Why This Change Was Made

Two concrete gaps in core code (independent of any specific context-engine plugin):

1. **No per-task timeout on the maintenance enqueue.** `scheduleDeferredTurnMaintenance` enqueues the maintenance worker via `enqueueCommandInLane(...)` with no options. The command queue already fully supports a per-task timeout (`taskTimeoutMs` → rejects with `CommandLaneTaskTimeoutError` and releases the lane), but the maintenance lane never set it — so a wedged run had no bound below the session-level abort.
2. **`session.stalled` had no log backoff.** `session.stuck` and `session.long_running` already throttle repeat warnings; `session.stalled` did not, so a persistently stalled session re-emitted a warn on every heartbeat.

## User Impact

- A wedged turn-maintenance run is now bounded in seconds (default 120s, configurable) and the lane is released, so a queued user message no longer waits for the session-level abort. On timeout the session stops treating maintenance as active and the queued turn proceeds.
- A persistently stalled session no longer floods the logs with a `session.stalled` warning every ~30s; the first warning is still immediate, and recovery is never gated by the backoff.
- No behavior change for healthy maintenance runs that finish well under the timeout.

### Changes

- Pass `taskTimeoutMs` to the deferred turn-maintenance enqueue, defaulting to `120000ms`, overridable via `agents.defaults.compaction.turnMaintenanceTaskTimeoutMs` (zod schema + type + help/labels). On timeout the enqueue rejects with `CommandLaneTaskTimeoutError`; the tracked-promise catch logs a `warn` with `sessionKey`/`taskId`/`taskTimeoutMs`, cancels the task descriptor, and runs the existing cleanup so the active-run entry is cleared and `waitForDeferredTurnMaintenanceForSession` resolves.
- Apply the existing `session.stuck`/`session.long_running` warn backoff (`max(last + threshold, last * 2)`) to `session.stalled`: first emission immediate, repeats throttled, and when throttled-but-recovery-eligible it still returns the classification so recovery is never gated.

> Note: `schedulerAbort` is intentionally **not** wired as `taskTimeoutAbortSignal` — in the queue that signal starts an abort-grace countdown and only fires on SIGINT/SIGTERM, so wiring it would impose the timeout as a shutdown grace and regress shutdown latency. `taskTimeoutMs` alone correctly bounds the wait.

## Evidence

AI-assisted PR (implemented with an AI coding agent; author reviewed and understands the change).

Local validation (all green) in this repo on this branch:

- `pnpm tsgo:core` — clean
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src` — clean
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/context-engine-maintenance.test.ts` — 23 passed (incl. 2 new: enqueue passes `taskTimeoutMs`; a wedged/never-resolving worker is bounded by the timeout)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts` — 73 passed (incl. 1 new: `session.stalled` backoff — first emits, immediate repeat suppressed, emits again after the window)
- `command-queue.test.ts`, `schema.help.quality.test.ts`, `config-misc.test.ts`, `diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts`, recovery runtime + integration tests — all passed
- `oxfmt --check` and oxlint on touched files — clean

`CHANGELOG.md` not touched (left for maintainers/ClawSweeper at landing).

Closes #96703
